title: OpenAI ships GPT-5 with native tool use and 1M context
description: OpenAI released GPT-5 with native tool use, 1M token context window, and significant improvements in reasoning and code generation. Available via API immediately. Pricing: $15/1M input tokens, $60/1M output.
actionNote: Worth benchmarking against Claude for LawTrades AI features. May offer cost advantages for high-volume contract parsing.

title: Vercel ships AI SDK 4.0 with streaming agents and persistent memory
description: Vercel's AI SDK 4.0 adds first-class support for streaming agent responses, persistent agent memory across sessions, and built-in tool orchestration. Works with Next.js 15 out of the box.
actionNote: Directly relevant to LawTrades AI features and The Marketplace frontend stack. Check compatibility with current Next.js version.

title: Anthropic releases Claude 4 Sonnet with 200K context and computer use v2
description: Anthropic shipped Claude 4 Sonnet, improving code generation by ~40% on SWE-bench and adding computer use v2 with better UI interaction accuracy. Context stays at 200K. Available on API now.
actionNote: This is Día's current model. Performance improvements in code gen directly benefit sub-agent coding tasks.

title: Linear ships Cycles v2 — AI sprint planning with automatic priority scoring
description: Linear released Cycles v2 with AI-powered sprint planning. The AI scores issue priority based on dependencies, blockers, team velocity, and due dates — then auto-populates cycles. Also adds a new 'Focus Mode' that hides all non-cycle issues.
actionNote: Ilolo uses Linear daily. Cycles v2 could meaningfully reduce sprint planning overhead. Worth demoing to the LawTrades team.

title: BigLaw firms rolling out autonomous AI agents for contract review — marketplace play for LawTrades
description: Several BigLaw firms (Kirkland, Latham, Skadden) announced autonomous AI agents for contract review and due diligence. Still requires senior attorney sign-off but the automation layer is here.
actionNote: Competitive intel — law firms are getting agentic AI. LawTrades should be the marketplace that connects this talent/tech to companies.

title: Cursor 3: Agent-first coding with parallel agents across cloud, SSH, and local
description: Cursor shipped v3.0 rebuilding its interface around AI agents. The new Agents Window lets devs run dozens of agents in parallel across local repos, cloud VMs, worktrees, and remote SSH.
actionNote: Ilolo uses Cursor daily — v3.0 is a big deal. Worth upgrading and exploring the Agents Window for LawTrades frontend work.

title: Microsoft open-sources Agent Governance Toolkit — covers all 10 OWASP Agentic risks
description: Microsoft released the Agent Governance Toolkit, a 7-package open-source system for governing autonomous AI agents: policy enforcement, zero-trust identity, execution sandboxing, and reliability engineering.
actionNote: Directly relevant to Ilolo's security focus and LawTrades AI. Worth reviewing for agentic features in the marketplace.

title: Polymarket arbitrage bot made $152K in 6 months on $30-50K capital — full anatomy
description: Deep analysis of a Polymarket bot (0x7a3f...) executing ~50,200 trades over 6 months, capturing avg 1.2% spread on Yes/No mispricings. Net profit ~$152K. 94.2% win rate. Stack: Python, py-clob-client, Polygon WebSocket.
actionNote: Core research for Ilolo's $100 Polymarket bot side project. Full research in workspace/research/polymarket-research.md

title: Columbia Law study: 'Informed' Polymarket traders netted $143M since 2024
description: Researchers analyzed 210,000+ Polymarket trades (2024-2026). 'Informed' traders netted $143M. Regulatory pressure incoming from CFTC and state AGs. Polymarket just banned insider trades but enforcement unclear.
actionNote: Stick to pure arb/market-making (risk-neutral) for lowest regulatory exposure.

title: Open-source oracle-lag-sniper: 61.4% win rate exploiting Polymarket's ~55-second Chainlink lag
description: JonathanPetersonn open-sourced a Python asyncio bot that exploits the ~55-second gap between Chainlink oracle updates and Polymarket order book repricing on 15-min BTC/ETH/XRP/SOL markets. 3 entry conditions: price moved >0.07% from open, >5min remaining, token price <$0.62. Backtested on 8,876 resolved markets with 61.4% win rate. Has live demo mode (no API keys needed).
actionNote: BEST starting point for Ilolo's $100 bot. Clone and run demo mode for 72h to validate signal, then fund. No sub-millisecond infrastructure needed — 55-sec window is forgiving.
